kit.en: a set of tools, equipment, or parts for a particular purpose

ex.en: She packed a first-aid kit for the camping trip.
ex.ko: 그녀는 캠핑 여행을 위해 구급상자를 챙겼다.

knock.en: hit a door or surface with your knuckles to attract attention

ex.en: Someone knocked on the door.
He knocked his head on the shelf.
Critics knocked the movie for its weak plot.
ex.ko: 평론가들은 그 영화의 약한 줄거리를 비판했다.

knowledge.en: information and understanding gained through learning or experience

ex.en: She has a deep knowledge of history.
ex.ko: 그녀는 역사에 대한 깊은 지식을 가지고 있다.

ex.en: Farming requires a lot of manual labor.
He labored for hours to finish the report.
ex.ko: 그는 보고서를 끝내려고 몇 시간 동안 애썼다.

labor.en: the process of giving birth to a baby

ex.en: She went into labor early in the morning.

lack.en: not have enough of something, or not have it at all

ex.en: The plant died because it lacked water.

lack.en: the state of not having enough of something

ex.en: The project failed due to a lack of funding.

lake.en: a large area of water surrounded by land

ex.en: They went fishing at the lake.

lamp.en: a device that produces light, often using electricity

ex.en: She turned on the lamp to read.

land.en: the solid part of the earth's surface, as opposed to the sea

ex.en: After weeks at sea, they finally saw land.
ex.ko: 몇 주간의 항해 끝에 그들은 마침내 육지를 보았다.

land.en: (of an aircraft) come down to the ground after flying

ex.en: The plane landed safely at the airport.
They dream of visiting a foreign land.
ex.ko: 그들은 낯선 나라를 방문하는 것을 꿈꾼다.

landscape.en: a wide view of an area of land, especially in the countryside

ex.en: The mountains created a beautiful landscape.

landscape.en: improve the appearance of an area of land by planting trees and flowers

ex.en: They hired someone to landscape the backyard.

lane.en: one of the strips a road is divided into for a single line of traffic

ex.en: Stay in your lane while driving.

lane.en: a narrow road, especially in the countryside

ex.en: We walked down a quiet country lane.

last.en: coming after all others; most recent

ex.en: This is the last chapter of the book.

last.en: continue for a period of time

ex.en: The meeting lasted two hours.
ex.ko: 그 회의는 두 시간 동안 지속되었다.

ex.en: When did you last see her?

latest.en: most recent or up to date

ex.en: Have you seen the latest news?

latest.en: the most recent information or development

ex.en: What's the latest on the project?

latter.en: referring to the second of two things or people just mentioned

ex.en: Of the two options, I prefer the latter one.
ex.ko: 두 가지 선택지 중에서 나는 후자를 선호한다.

latter.en: the second of two things or people just mentioned

ex.en: The latter was more convincing.

law.en: the system of rules that a country or community recognizes as binding

ex.en: It is against the law to steal.

law.en: the subject or profession of studying and practicing legal rules

ex.en: She is studying law at university.

lawyer.en: a person who is trained to advise people about the law

ex.en: He hired a lawyer to handle the case.
ex.ko: 그는 그 사건을 처리하기 위해 변호사를 고용했다.

laziness.en: the quality of being unwilling to work or make an effort

ex.en: His laziness caused him to fail the class.
ex.ko: 그의 게으름 때문에 그는 수업에서 낙제했다.

lead.en: guide or direct people or a group towards a place or goal

ex.en: She led the tourists through the museum.

lead.en: the position of being ahead of others in a race or competition

ex.en: Our team took the lead in the second half.

lead.en: having the most important role or position

ex.en: She played the lead role in the school play.

learner.en: a person who is learning a subject or skill

ex.en: This app is designed for English learners.
ex.ko: 이 앱은 영어 학습자를 위해 만들어졌다.

least.en: smallest in amount, extent, or degree

ex.en: This option costs the least money.
ex.ko: 이 선택지가 가장 적은 비용이 든다.

leather.en: material made from the skin of an animal

ex.en: He bought a leather jacket.

left-hand.en: on or towards the left side

ex.en: Take the left-hand turn at the intersection.

leisure.en: free time when you are not working and can relax

ex.en: She spends her leisure time reading novels.
ex.ko: 그녀는 여가 시간에 소설을 읽으며 보낸다.

lemon.en: a yellow citrus fruit with a sour taste

ex.en: She squeezed a lemon into her tea.

lemonade.en: a sweet drink made from lemon juice, sugar, and water

ex.en: We drank cold lemonade on a hot day.
ex.ko: 우리는 더운 날 시원한 레모네이드를 마셨다.

lend.en: give something to someone for a period of time, expecting it back

ex.en: Can you lend me your pen?
I have less time than I thought.

level.en: the amount, standard, or grade of something

ex.en: Her English is at an advanced level.

level.en: having a flat, even surface with no part higher than another

ex.en: Make sure the shelf is level before you fix it.

level.en: aim a weapon or criticism at someone or something

ex.en: He leveled the gun at the target.

liberty.cue: 억압받지 않고 스스로 선택할 수 있는 상태
liberty.en: the freedom to live, think, and act as you choose

ex.en: They fought for liberty and independence.

license.en: an official document giving permission to do, use, or own something

ex.en: She got her driver's license last week.

license.en: give official permission for someone to do or use something

ex.en: The city licensed the restaurant to sell alcohol.
ex.ko: 시는 그 식당에 주류 판매를 허가했다.

lie.en: say something that is not true

ex.en: He lied about his age.

lie.en: a statement that you know is not true

ex.en: She told a lie to avoid trouble.

lie.en: be in or move into a flat position on a surface

ex.en: He lay on the bed and closed his eyes.

lifestyle.en: the way in which a person or group lives

ex.en: They have a very healthy lifestyle.
ex.ko: 그들은 매우 건강한 생활 방식을 가지고 있다.

ex.en: It's likely to rain this afternoon.
ex.ko: 오늘 오후에 비가 올 것 같다.

lip.en: either of the two soft edges of the mouth

ex.en: She bit her lip nervously.
Buy two liters of milk.
The children were lively and cheerful.

load.en: put a large amount of something into or onto a vehicle or container

ex.en: They loaded the boxes onto the truck.

load.en: a heavy or large amount of something that is carried or transported

ex.en: The truck was carrying a heavy load.
ex.ko: 그 트럭은 무거운 짐을 싣고 있었다.

load.en: transfer data or a program into a computer's memory

ex.en: The website took a while to load.
ex.ko: 그 웹사이트가 로딩되는 데 시간이 좀 걸렸다.

local.en: relating to a particular area or neighborhood

ex.en: We shop at the local market.

lock.en: fasten a door or container with a key or bolt

ex.en: Don't forget to lock the door.

lock.en: a device used to fasten a door or container so it can only be opened with a key

ex.en: She changed the lock on the front door.

logical.en: following the rules of logic; reasonable

ex.en: That's a logical explanation for the problem.
ex.ko: 그것은 그 문제에 대한 논리적인 설명이다.

loose.en: not firmly fixed or fitting tightly

ex.en: This shirt is too loose for me.

lose.en: no longer have something because it has been taken away or misplaced

ex.en: I lost my keys yesterday.

lose.en: fail to win a game or competition

ex.en: Our team lost the match 2-1.

lost.en: unable to find your way or know where you are

ex.en: We got lost in the old part of the city.
ex.ko: 우리는 도시의 옛 지역에서 길을 잃었다.

lost.en: no longer possessed, or unable to be found

ex.en: She never found her lost wallet.
ex.ko: 그녀는 잃어버린 지갑을 결국 찾지 못했다.

loudly.en: with a lot of noise or volume

ex.en: He laughed loudly at the joke.

lover.en: a person that someone is in a romantic relationship with

ex.en: They have been lovers for three years.

lover.en: a person who likes or enjoys something very much

ex.en: He is a great lover of classical music.
ex.ko: 그는 클래식 음악을 아주 좋아하는 애호가이다.

low.en: not high in level, amount, or position

ex.en: The temperature is very low today.
He spoke in a low voice.

luckily.en: in a way that shows good fortune

ex.en: Luckily, no one was hurt in the accident.
ex.ko: 다행히 그 사고에서 다친 사람은 아무도 없었다.

lunchtime.en: the time of day when people usually eat lunch

ex.en: Let's meet at lunchtime.
She wrote the lyrics for the new song.
She was mad at him for being late.

mad.en: mentally ill or behaving in a very strange way

ex.en: He must be mad to drive in this storm.
ex.ko: 이 폭풍 속에서 운전하다니 그는 미친 게 틀림없다.

magic.en: a special power that makes impossible things happen

ex.en: In the story, the wizard uses magic to help the villagers.
ex.ko: 이야기 속에서 마법사는 마법을 사용해 마을 사람들을 돕는다.

magic.en: clever tricks performed for entertainment that seem impossible

ex.en: The magician performed magic tricks at the party.

main course.en: the largest and most important dish of a meal

ex.en: For the main course, we had grilled chicken.
ex.ko: 메인 요리로 우리는 구운 치킨을 먹었다.

mainly.en: more than anything else; for the most part

ex.en: The book is mainly about her childhood.
ex.ko: 그 책은 주로 그녀의 어린 시절에 관한 것이다.

major.en: an army officer of a rank above captain

ex.en: He was promoted to major last year.

major.en: the main subject that a student specializes in at college or university

ex.en: Her major is computer science.
Traffic is a major problem in this city.
ex.ko: 교통 체증은 이 도시의 주요한 문제이다.

make-up.en: cosmetics used to color and enhance the face

ex.en: She wore light make-up for the interview.
ex.ko: 그녀는 면접을 위해 옅은 화장을 했다.

maker.en: a person or company that makes or produces something

ex.en: The company is a major maker of electronics.

male.en: of or relating to men or male animals

ex.en: The doctor examined the male patient.

male.en: a man or a male animal

ex.en: Only males have this feature in this species.
ex.ko: 이 종에서는 수컷만이 이 특징을 가진다.

mall.cue: 여러 상점이 모여 있는 실내 건물
mall.en: a large building or group of buildings containing many shops

ex.en: We spent the afternoon at the mall.

manage.en: be in charge of or control a business, team, or activity

ex.en: She manages a team of ten people.
ex.ko: 그녀는 열 명으로 구성된 팀을 관리한다.

ex.en: He managed to finish the race despite the rain.

manager.en: a person who is responsible for controlling or running a business, team, or department

ex.en: He was promoted to manager last month.

manner.en: the way in which something is done or happens

ex.en: She smiled in a friendly manner.
It's good manners to say thank you.

mark.en: a number or letter that shows how well someone has done in a test or piece of work

ex.en: She got a high mark on the exam.

mark.en: a visible sign or spot on a surface

ex.en: There's a mark on the wall.

mark.en: put a symbol on something, or check and score someone's work

ex.en: The teacher marked all the tests over the weekend.
ex.ko: 선생님은 주말 동안 모든 시험지를 채점했다.

market.en: promote and sell a product or service

ex.en: The company markets its products worldwide.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 전 세계에 제품을 판매한다.

market.en: a place where people buy and sell goods

ex.en: We bought fresh vegetables at the market.
They have been married for ten years.

marry.en: become the husband or wife of someone

ex.en: They got married last spring.
We had a marvelous time at the party.
ex.ko: 우리는 파티에서 정말 훌륭한 시간을 보냈다.

material.en: the substance or substances that something is made from

ex.en: This bag is made of recycled material.

material.en: information or ideas that can be used to create or complete something

ex.en: She collected material for her research paper.
ex.ko: 그녀는 연구 논문을 위한 자료를 수집했다.

max.en: the greatest possible amount or degree (informal)

ex.en: The speed limit here is 60 km/h max.

meaning.en: what something means or is intended to express

ex.en: What is the meaning of this word?

medal.en: a small metal disk given as an award for an achievement

ex.en: She won a gold medal at the Olympics.

medical.en: relating to the treatment of illness and injuries

ex.en: She works in the medical field.

melon.cue: 과육이 달고 즙이 많은 큰 과일
melon.en: a large, round, sweet fruit with juicy flesh

ex.en: We ate cold melon in the summer.

member.en: a person who belongs to a group or organization

ex.en: She is a member of the tennis club.

mention.en: talk about something briefly, without giving many details

ex.en: She mentioned that she might be late.

mention.en: a brief remark about something or someone

ex.en: There was no mention of the accident in the news.
ex.ko: 뉴스에서 그 사고에 대한 언급은 없었다.

menu.en: a list of the food available in a restaurant

ex.en: Can I see the menu, please?

menu.en: a list of options shown on a computer or phone screen

ex.en: Select 'settings' from the menu.

metal.en: a hard, shiny material such as iron, gold, or steel

ex.en: The bridge is made of metal.

meter.en: a metric unit of length equal to 100 centimeters

ex.en: The room is five meters wide.

meter.en: a device that measures and records the amount of something, such as gas or electricity

ex.en: He checked the electricity meter.

method.en: a particular, especially systematic, way of doing something

ex.en: They tried a new method to solve the problem.
ex.ko: 그들은 그 문제를 풀기 위해 새로운 방법을 시도했다.

midday.en: twelve o'clock in the middle of the day

ex.en: We met at midday for lunch.
The party ended at midnight.

might.en: used to say that something is possible

ex.en: It might rain later.
The army fought with all its might.

military.en: relating to soldiers, armies, or war

ex.en: He has a military background.

military.en: the armed forces of a country

ex.en: The military was deployed to help after the storm.
ex.ko: 폭풍 후 지원을 위해 군대가 파견되었다.

ex.en: The city has a population of two million.

mineral water.cue: 미네랄 성분이 든 병에 담긴 물
mineral water.en: water that naturally contains minerals, often sold in bottles

ex.en: He ordered a bottle of mineral water.

mirror.en: a piece of glass that reflects an image

ex.en: She looked at herself in the mirror.
The novel mirrors the author's own life.
ex.ko: 그 소설은 작가 자신의 삶을 반영한다.

missing.en: not in the place where it should be, or unable to be found

ex.en: My phone is missing from my bag.

mistake.en: an action or decision that is wrong or produces an unwanted result

ex.en: I made a mistake on the test.

mistake.en: fail to understand or recognize something or someone correctly

ex.en: I mistook him for his brother.
Mix the flour and sugar together.
The dessert is a mix of fruit and cream.
ex.ko: 그 디저트는 과일과 크림이 섞인 것이다.

model.en: a small copy of something, or a particular type or version of a product

ex.en: He built a model airplane.

model.en: a person who displays clothes or poses for photographs or paintings

ex.en: She works as a fashion model.

model.en: deserving to be copied because it is an excellent example

ex.en: He is a model student.

modern.en: belonging to the present time or a recent time, rather than the past

ex.en: They live in a modern apartment.

mood.en: the way someone feels at a particular time

ex.en: She's been in a good mood all day.
They pray at the mosque every Friday.

mostly.en: in most cases; for the most part

ex.en: The audience was mostly students.

motorway.en: a wide road built for fast, long-distance traffic

ex.en: We drove down the motorway to London.

mug.en: a large cup with a handle, used for hot drinks

ex.en: He drank his coffee from a big mug.

mug.en: attack and rob someone in a public place

ex.en: She was mugged on her way home.
ex.ko: 그녀는 집에 가는 길에 강도를 당했다.

ex.en: He was arrested for murdering his neighbor.

murder.en: the crime of deliberately killing someone

ex.en: The police are investigating a murder.

museum.en: a building where objects of historical, scientific, or artistic value are displayed

ex.en: We visited the art museum on Saturday.

mushroom.cue: 숲이나 들판에서 자라는 갓 모양의 균류
mushroom.en: a fungus with a rounded cap on a stem, some of which are edible

ex.en: She added mushrooms to the soup.
The number of users mushroomed overnight.

musical.en: a play or film that includes singing and dancing

ex.en: We watched a musical on Broadway.

musical.en: relating to or good at music

ex.en: She comes from a musical family.

myself.en: used to refer to the speaker as the object of a verb, or for emphasis

ex.en: I made this cake myself.

mysterious.en: difficult to understand or explain; strange or unknown

ex.en: He disappeared in mysterious circumstances.

mystery.en: something that is difficult or impossible to understand or explain

ex.en: The disappearance remains a mystery.
ex.ko: 그 실종은 여전히 미스터리로 남아 있다.

mystery.en: a story about a crime that is solved at the end

ex.en: She loves reading mystery novels.
ex.ko: 그녀는 추리 소설 읽는 것을 좋아한다.

napkin.cue: 식사할 때 입을 닦는 천이나 종이
napkin.en: a piece of cloth or paper used at meals to wipe your mouth or hands

ex.en: He wiped his mouth with a napkin.

nation.en: a country, especially considered as a group of people with its own government and land

ex.en: Many nations signed the agreement.

national.en: relating to a whole nation or country, rather than to part of it or to other countries

ex.en: It was announced on national television.

national.en: a citizen of a particular country

ex.en: All foreign nationals must register.

native.en: connected with the place where you were born

ex.en: English is her native language.

native.en: a person born in a particular place

ex.en: He is a native of Seoul.

natural.en: existing in nature; not made or caused by people

ex.en: Honey is a natural sweetener.
It's natural to feel nervous before an exam.
ex.ko: 시험 전에 긴장하는 것은 자연스러운 일이다.

nature.en: the physical world, including plants, animals, and landscapes, as opposed to human creations

ex.en: They enjoy hiking and exploring nature.
ex.ko: 그들은 하이킹을 하며 자연을 탐험하는 것을 즐긴다.

nature.en: the basic character or qualities of a person or thing

ex.en: It's not in his nature to lie.
It's nearly midnight.

necessary.en: needed in order to achieve a certain result or effect

ex.en: Water is necessary for life.

negative.en: expressing disapproval, or having a bad or harmful effect

ex.en: She has a negative attitude about the plan.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 계획에 대해 부정적인 태도를 가지고 있다.

negative.en: (of a medical test) showing that a particular condition is not present

ex.en: His test result was negative.
The temperature dropped to negative five degrees.
She felt nervous before the interview.

nervous.en: relating to the nerves or nervous system

ex.en: The nervous system controls the body's responses.

net.en: material made of crossed strings or wires with spaces between them

ex.en: The fisherman threw his net into the water.
I found the information on the net.

net.en: remaining after all deductions, such as tax

ex.en: Her net income is $3,000 a month.

next.en: coming immediately after the present one in time or order

ex.en: See you next week.
What happened next?

next to.en: in a position right beside something or someone

ex.en: The bank is next to the post office.
No one answered the phone.

nobody.en: not a single person; no one

ex.en: Nobody knew the answer.

noisy.en: making a lot of loud, unpleasant sound

ex.en: The street outside is very noisy.

noon.en: twelve o'clock in the middle of the day

ex.en: The store opens at noon.

normal.en: usual, average, or expected; not unusual

ex.en: It's normal to feel tired after a long trip.
ex.ko: 긴 여행 후에 피곤한 것은 정상적인 일이다.

north.en: the direction that is on your left when you face the rising sun

ex.en: The wind is blowing from the north.

north.en: situated in or facing the north

ex.en: They live on the north side of the city.
The birds fly north in spring.

notice.en: become aware of something or someone

ex.en: I noticed a strange smell in the kitchen.

notice.en: a written or printed announcement giving information

ex.en: There's a notice on the board about the meeting.

noun.en: a word that refers to a person, place, thing, or idea

ex.en: 'Dog' and 'happiness' are both nouns.

novel.en: a long written story about imaginary people and events

ex.en: She wrote her first novel at twenty.
ex.ko: 그녀는 스무 살에 첫 소설을 썼다.

novel.en: new and original, not like anything seen before

ex.en: He came up with a novel solution to the problem.
ex.ko: 그는 그 문제에 대한 참신한 해결책을 생각해냈다.

nowadays.en: at the present time, in contrast with the past

ex.en: Nowadays, most people use smartphones.

oak.en: a large tree that produces acorns and hard wood

ex.en: The table is made of solid oak.
Please state your occupation on the form.

occupation.en: control of a country or area by a foreign military force

ex.en: The country was under foreign occupation for decades.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 수십 년간 외국의 점령 아래 있었다.

offer.en: present something for someone to accept or refuse

ex.en: He offered me a cup of coffee.
ex.ko: 그는 나에게 커피 한 잔을 권했다.

offer.en: something that is offered, such as a suggestion or a price

ex.en: She accepted the job offer.

official.en: a person who holds a position of authority in an organization or government

ex.en: A government official gave a speech.

official.en: approved by an authority and made publicly known

ex.en: The official announcement will be made tomorrow.

oil.en: a thick liquid used in cooking, made from plants or animals

ex.en: She fried the vegetables in oil.

oil.en: a thick, dark liquid found underground that is used as fuel

ex.en: The country's economy depends on oil exports.
ex.ko: 그 나라의 경제는 석유 수출에 의존한다.

ex.en: She dressed the salad with olive oil.

omelet.en: a dish made from beaten eggs cooked in a pan

ex.en: He made a cheese omelet for breakfast.

onion.en: a round vegetable with a strong smell and taste, used in cooking

ex.en: Chop the onion finely.

online.en: connected to or available through the internet

ex.en: She took an online course.
I bought these shoes online.
This machine operates automatically.

operate.en: control or manage a machine, business, or system

ex.en: He knows how to operate the new software.
ex.ko: 그는 새 소프트웨어를 조작하는 법을 안다.

ex.en: The surgeon operated on her knee.

opinion.en: a personal belief or judgment about something

ex.en: What's your opinion on this issue?
ex.ko: 이 문제에 대한 네 의견은 뭐야?

opportunity.cue: 무언가를 할 수 있는 좋은 상황
opportunity.en: a chance to do something, especially something you want or need to do

ex.en: Studying abroad gave her a great opportunity.
ex.ko: 해외 유학은 그녀에게 좋은 기회를 주었다.

oppose.en: disagree with or be against a plan, idea, or action

ex.en: Many people opposed the new law.
ex.ko: 많은 사람들이 그 새 법에 반대했다.

opposite.en: in a position facing someone or something else

ex.en: She sat opposite me at the table.
They walked in opposite directions.

opposite.en: a word or thing that is completely different from another

ex.en: 'Hot' is the opposite of 'cold'.

organize.en: plan and arrange an event or activity

ex.en: They organized a surprise party for her.
ex.ko: 그들은 그녀를 위해 깜짝 파티를 준비했다.

organize.en: arrange things into a clear or logical order

ex.en: He organized his files by date.

organized.en: arranged in a clear, logical, or efficient way

ex.en: She is a very organized person.

original.en: existing from the beginning; the first of its kind

ex.en: This is the original version of the song.

original.en: the first form of something, from which copies are made

ex.en: Keep the original and send a copy.

original.en: new and imaginative; not copied from anything else

ex.en: She had a very original idea for the project.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 프로젝트에 대해 매우 독창적인 아이디어를 냈다.

orphan.en: a child whose parents have died

ex.en: The orphan was raised by his grandparents.

orphan.en: cause a child to become an orphan

ex.en: The war orphaned thousands of children.
ex.ko: 그 전쟁은 수천 명의 아이들을 고아로 만들었다.

ourselves.en: used to refer to the speakers as the object of a verb, or for emphasis

ex.en: We cooked dinner ourselves.

out of.en: moving away from the inside of a place

ex.en: She walked out of the room.

out of.en: used to show a part taken from a whole group or amount

ex.en: Nine out of ten people liked the movie.

oven.en: a kitchen appliance used for baking or roasting food

ex.en: She put the cake in the oven.

overseas.en: in, to, or from a foreign country across the sea

ex.en: She has an overseas job offer.

overseas.en: to or in a foreign country across the sea

ex.en: He moved overseas last year.

overweight.en: weighing more than is healthy or normal

ex.en: The doctor said he was slightly overweight.

pacific.en: relating to or bordering the Pacific Ocean

ex.en: Many islands are located in the Pacific region.
ex.ko: 많은 섬들이 태평양 지역에 위치해 있다.

ex.en: He is known for his pacific nature.

pack.en: put things into a bag or container, especially for a trip

ex.en: She packed her suitcase for the trip.
ex.ko: 그녀는 여행을 위해 여행 가방을 쌌다.

pack.en: a set of things sold or packaged together

ex.en: He bought a pack of batteries.

pack.en: a group of animals that hunt together, such as wolves

ex.en: A pack of wolves roamed the forest.
She trained hard for the Olympic Games.
ex.ko: 그녀는 올림픽 경기를 위해 열심히 훈련했다.
